title: Calceolaria sessiliflora
intro: Calceolaria sessiliflora, also known as the Slipper Flower, is a flowering plant species native to South America, characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers and fragrant, minty aroma. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calceolaria sessiliflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Calceolaria sessiliflora is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory issues.
